content: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is brain dysfunction from external mechanical force, affecting 2.5 million Americans annually. Classification by severity: mild (GCS 13-15, concussion), moderate (GCS 9-12), severe (GCS 3-8). Primary injury occurs at impact (contusion, hemorrhage, diffuse axonal injury); secondary injury evolves hours-days later (ischemia, edema, excitotoxicity, inflammation). Mechanisms: closed head injury (acceleration-deceleration, rotational forces causing DAI), penetrating trauma, blast injury. Concussion is functional disturbance without structural damage on CT/MRI—symptoms include headache, dizziness, confusion, amnesia, nausea, photophobia. Post-concussion syndrome: persistent symptoms >4 weeks. Complications: epidural hematoma (lucid interval, lens-shaped on CT), subdural hematoma (venous tearing, crescent-shaped), subarachnoid hemorrhage, cerebral edema, increased ICP, post-traumatic epilepsy, chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) from repetitive injury.
